sentence: O tempo não está nada bom hoje de manhã.
ipa: u ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈsta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ ˈoʒe de maˈɲɐ̃
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all this morning.
notes: Curitibano: final /e/ kept as [e] in 'hoje' (the Curitiba 'e/o fechados'), not raised to [i] (silva2002; noll2008); palatal [ɲ] in 'manhã' (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Estes chinelos custam quarenta reais aqui.
ipa: ˈestis ʃiˈnelus ˈkustɐ̃w̃ kwaˈɾẽtɐ heˈajs aˈki
gloss_en: These sandals cost forty reais here.
notes: Guttural onset rhotic [h] in 'reais' — the Paulistano-type strong R of transitional Curitibano, unlike the Gaúcho trill (noll2008); alveolar coda /s/ in 'estes'/'reais' (silva2002).

sentence: A consulta é amanhã às três da tarde.
ipa: ɐ kõˈsuwtɐ ˈɛ amaˈɲɐ̃ as ˈtɾes dɐ ˈtaɾde
gloss_en: The appointment is tomorrow at three in the afternoon.
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'amanhã' (mateus_dandrade2000); coda /l/ vocalised to [w] in 'consulta' (silva2002); alveolar coda /s/ in 'às'/'três' (callou_leite2001).

sentence: Meu irmão mora perto do mercado.
ipa: ˈmew iˈɾmɐ̃w̃ ˈmɔɾɐ ˈpeɾtu du meˈɾkadu
gloss_en: My brother lives near the market.
notes: Nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'irmão' (mateus_dandrade2000); coda /r/ tap [ɾ] in 'perto'/'mercado', not retroflex — the retroflex caipira r belongs to the Paraná interior, not Curitiba (amaral1920); final /o/ kept close (noll2008).

sentence: Siga reto e vire na próxima esquina.
ipa: ˈsiɡɐ ˈhetu e ˈviɾe nɐ ˈpɾɔʃimɐ eˈskinɐ
gloss_en: Go straight and turn at the next corner.
notes: Guttural onset [h] in 'reto' (noll2008); open-mid [ɔ] in 'próxima' (mateus_dandrade2000); 'reto e' vowel-junction sandhi (callou_leite2001).

sentence: A vina com pão estava muito boa.
ipa: ɐ ˈvinɐ kõ ˈpɐ̃w̃ eˈstavɐ ˈmujtu ˈboɐ
gloss_en: The sausage with bread was very good.
notes: Curitibano regional lexicon 'vina' (Vienna sausage), from the German/Slavic settler substratum (noll2008); nasal [õ] in 'com', nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'pão' (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Onde é que você comprou esse guarda-chuva?
ipa: ˈõde ˈɛ ke voˈs̺e kõˈpɾow ˈese ˈɡwaɾdɐ ˈʃuvɐ
gloss_en: Where did you buy that umbrella?
notes: Prestige pronoun 'você' (not 'tu'), the Curitibano default (noll2008); [ow] in 'comprou' (mateus_dandrade2000); 'que você' and open-mid [ɛ] in 'é' (callou_leite2001).

sentence: Quantos piás vieram jogar bola hoje?
ipa: kwɐ̃ˈtus piˈas viˈeɾɐ̃w̃ ʒoˈɡaɾ ˈbolɐ ˈoʒe
gloss_en: How many kids came to play football today?
notes: Curitibano/Paranaense regional lexicon 'piá' (boy/kid), from the Tupi-Guarani-influenced southern substratum (noll2008); final /e/ kept as [e] in 'hoje' (silva2002).

sentence: Se você quisesse, comprava aquela casa.
ipa: se voˈs̺e kiˈzese kõˈpɾavɐ aˈkelɐ ˈkazɐ
gloss_en: If you wanted, I would buy that house.
notes: 'você' + 3sg imperfect subjunctive 'quisesse' (noll2008); reduced-apodosis conditional with imperfect 'comprava', colloquial BR (callou_leite2001); dental /d/ retained (silva2002).

sentence: Você comprou as frutas e as verduras na feira?
ipa: voˈs̺e kõˈpɾow as ˈfɾutas e as veˈɾduɾas nɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: Did you buy the fruit and the vegetables at the market?
notes: 'você' paradigm (noll2008); [ow] in 'comprou' (mateus_dandrade2000); alveolar coda /s/ across 'as frutas'/'as verduras', no palatalisation (silva2002).

sentence: Amanhã nós vamos de carro até o litoral.
ipa: amaˈɲɐ̃ ˈnɔs ˈvamus de ˈkaʁu aˈtɛ u litoˈɾaw
gloss_en: Tomorrow we are going by car to the coast.
notes: Guttural [ʁ] in 'carro' vs coda /l/ vocalised to [w] in 'litoral' (noll2008); palatal [ɲ] and nasal in 'amanhã', open-mid [ɔ] in 'nós' (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Me liga quando você chegar em casa.
ipa: me ˈliɡɐ ˈkwɐ̃du voˈs̺e ʃeˈɡaɾ ẽj̃ ˈkazɐ
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: 'você' + 3sg future subjunctive 'chegar' (noll2008); nasal [ẽ] in 'em', dental /d/ in 'quando' (silva2002).

sentence: Bom dia, e daí, como você tem passado?
ipa: ˈbõ ˈdiɐ e ˈdai ˈkomu voˈs̺e ˈtẽj̃ paˈsadu
gloss_en: Good morning, so, how have you been?
notes: Curitibano discourse marker 'e daí' (noll2008); dental /d/ before /i/ retained in 'dia'/'daí', no affrication (silva2002); nasal [õ]/[ẽ] in 'bom'/'tem' (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: Mais vale um pássaro na mão que dois voando.
ipa: ˈmajs ˈvale ˈũ ˈpasaɾu nɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ ke ˈdojs voˈɐ̃du
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Proverb in neutral register; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'mão' (mateus_dandrade2000); coda /s/ [s] in 'mais'/'dois' (silva2002).

sentence: O carro do Ricardo enguiçou na estrada.
ipa: u ˈkaʁu du hiˈkaɾdu ẽɡiˈsow nɐ eˈstɾadɐ
gloss_en: Ricardo's car broke down on the road.
notes: Guttural strong R: [ʁ] in intervocalic 'carro' and [h] in onset 'Ricardo' — the Curitibano rhotic (noll2008); [ow] in 'enguiçou' (mateus_dandrade2000).

sentence: O leiteiro trouxe pouco leite outro dia.
ipa: u leˈjtejɾu ˈtɾowse ˈpowku ˈlejte ˈowtɾu ˈdiɐ
gloss_en: The milkman brought little milk the other day.
notes: Retained falling diphthongs [ej]/[ow] in 'leiteiro'/'pouco'/'outro' (mateus_dandrade2000); final /e/ kept as [e] in 'leite', the Curitiba closed vowel (silva2002).

sentence: O sol forte esquentava o canavial.
ipa: u ˈsɔw ˈfɔɾte eskẽˈtavɐ u kanaviˈaw
gloss_en: The strong sun heated the sugarcane field.
notes: Coda /l/ vocalised to [w] in 'sol'/'canavial' (silva2002); tap [ɾ] coda in 'forte' (callou_leite2001); close-mid vowels (noll2008).

sentence: A filha da vizinha brincava no ninho de palha.
ipa: ɐ ˈfiʎɐ dɐ viˈziɲɐ bɾĩˈkavɐ nu ˈniɲu de ˈpaʎɐ
gloss_en: The neighbour's daughter played by the straw nest.
notes: Palatals [ʎ] ('filha','palha') and [ɲ] ('vizinha','ninho') (mateus_dandrade2000); dental /d/ in 'da'/'de' (silva2002).

sentence: O café estava ótimo, mas a sopa ficou fria.
ipa: u kaˈfɛ eˈstavɐ ˈɔtimu ˈmas ɐ ˈsopɐ fiˈkow ˈfɾiɐ
gloss_en: The coffee was great, but the soup came out cold.
notes: Open-mids [ɛ] ('café') and [ɔ] ('ótimo') (mateus_dandrade2000); [ow] in 'ficou' (callou_leite2001).

sentence: Quem não tem cão caça com gato, dizia o velho.
ipa: ˈkẽj̃ ˈnɐ̃w̃ ˈtẽj̃ ˈkɐ̃w̃ ˈkasɐ kõ ˈɡatu diˈziɐ u ˈveʎu
gloss_en: Who has no dog hunts with a cat, the old man used to say.
notes: Nasal diphthongs [ɐ̃w̃] in 'não'/'cão' (mateus_dandrade2000); palatal [ʎ] in 'velho' (silva2002); dental /d/ before /i/ in 'dizia' (callou_leite2001).
